description: Subclass changes the expected behavior of the parent dict's access method.
reasoning: Divergent Change smell -> LSP violation because LookupDict inherits from 'dict' but overrides __getitem__ to return None for missing keys instead of raising a KeyError. This breaks the fundamental contract of the Python dictionary interface, where consumers expect a KeyError when accessing a non-existent key via the bracket operator [].

description: Subclass overrides behavior in a way that breaks the base class contract.
reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because HTTPProxyAuth inherits from HTTPBasicAuth but changes the side effect of the __call__ method. While HTTPBasicAuth is contracted to set the 'Authorization' header, HTTPProxyAuth sets the 'Proxy-Authorization' header instead. A component expecting an HTTPBasicAuth object to authorize a request will find the 'Authorization' header missing if passed an HTTPProxyAuth instance.

description: Subclass nullifies a parent class method by raising NotImplementedError.
reasoning: Refused Bequest smell -> LSP violation because MockRequest explicitly mimics the urllib2.Request interface but raises NotImplementedError for the add_header method. This violates the substitution principle where a subtype (the mock) should be able to stand in for the base type (the real request) without throwing unexpected errors for standard interface methods.

description: Subclass violates postconditions and exception contracts of the Mapping interface.
reasoning: Behavioral Change smell -> LSP violation because RequestsCookieJar implements the MutableMapping interface but breaks its contract in two ways: 1) __getitem__ (via _find_no_duplicates) raises a custom CookieConflictError (RuntimeError) instead of a KeyError or returning a value, which is unexpected for standard mapping consumers. 2) __setitem__ (via set) deletes a key if the value is None, whereas the Mapping contract requires that setting a key to None should result in the key existing with a None value.
